② 先生ほど — comparison marker; “as much as (that) teacher,” sets the ほど…ない frame.  
④ わかりやすく教えてくれる — relative clause; “teaches in an easy-to-understand way,” modifies 先生.  
① 先生は — topic marker on the head noun; sets up “such a teacher” as the topic for いない.

Reconstructed Sentence  
私は、森先生の授業を受けてから数学が好きになった。あの先生ほどわかりやすく教えてくれる先生はいないと思う。  
→ Since taking Professor Mori’s class, I’ve come to like math. I think there isn’t any teacher who teaches as clearly as that teacher.

Why This Order Works  
- あの attaches to 先生 to identify the benchmark person.  
- 先生ほど establishes the comparison standard in the ほど…ない pattern.  
- わかりやすく教えてくれる modifies 先生, describing the kind of teacher.  
- 先生は + いない completes “There is no such teacher,” making a natural comparative-negative: Xほど…先生はいない.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 (★자리=두 번째 「先生ほど」). 올바른 순서: あの→先生ほど→わかりやすく教えてくれる→先生は.
포인트: 「Nほど〜ない」(N만큼 ~하지 않다)의 최상급 표현. 「あの先生ほどわかりやすく教えてくれる先生はいない」(저 선생님만큼 알기 쉽게 가르쳐 주는 선생님은 없다).

questions.stem: 「今日は、コーラを使った鶏肉の煮物を紹介します。鶏肉は____　____　★　____知っていますか。」
questions.expl:
**Answer (★ Position)**  
Answer 2: 柔らかく

**Correct Order**  
3 → 1 → 2 → 4  
📌 コーラで → 煮ることで → 柔らかく → なるのを

**Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown**  
③ コーラで — “with cola”; indicates means  
① 煮ることで — “by simmering”; indicates method  
② 柔らかく — “soft”; adverbial form  
④ なるのを — “the becoming (soft)”; nominalized form

**Reconstructed Sentence**  
「今日は、コーラを使った鶏肉の煮物を紹介します。鶏肉は**コーラで煮ることで柔らかくなるのを**知っていますか。」  
→ “Today, I’ll introduce a dish of chicken simmered with cola. Did you know that simmering chicken in cola makes it tender?”

**Why This Order Works**  
- “コーラで煮ることで” expresses the cause or method.  
- “柔らかくなる” describes the result.  
- The phrase “〜のを知っていますか” asks if the listener knows that fact.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 (★자리=세 번째 「柔(やわ)らかく」). 올바른 순서: コーラで→煮ることで→柔らかく→なるのを.
포인트: 「Vることで」(V함으로써, 수단)+「〜く+なる」(~게 되다). 「コーラで煮ることで柔らかくなるのを知っていますか」(콜라로 끓이면 부드러워지는 것을 아세요?).

questions.stem: 山下「今度、北森町に行くんだけれど、北森町のレストランでどこかいいところ、知ってる？」南「私はあまり知らないんだけれど、田中さん____　____　★　____どう？」
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)  
Answer 3: から

Correct Order  
1 → 4 → 3 → 2  
📌 が → 北森町に住んでいる → から → 聞いてみたら

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown  
① が — subject marker; marks 田中さん as the subject of 住んでいる  
④ 北森町に住んでいる — “lives in Kitamori”; reason clause content  
③ から — “because/since”; links the reason to the suggestion that follows  
② 聞いてみたら — “why don’t you try asking?”; tara + どう forms a suggestion

Reconstructed Sentence  
私はあまり知らないんだけれど、田中さんが北森町に住んでいるから、聞いてみたらどう？  
→ “I don’t really know, but since Tanaka lives in Kitamori, how about asking him?”

Why This Order Works  
- が marks Tanaka as the subject of the reason clause.  
- から must follow the full reason clause (田中さんが北森町に住んでいる).  
- 聞いてみたらどう closes with a natural suggestion.  
- Star position is third (___ ___ ★ ___), so から (Answer 3) goes in the third slot.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 (★자리=세 번째 「から」). 올바른 순서: が→北森町に住んでいる→から→聞いてみたら.
포인트: 「Nから」(N에게서, 원인·출처)+「〜たらどう？」(~하는 게 어때?). 「田中さんが北森町に住んでいるから聞いてみたらどう」(다나카 씨가 살고 있으니 물어보면 어때).

questions.stem: 林「タンさんは、夏休みに国へ帰りますか。」タン「いいえ、今年は帰りません。日本で____　____　★　____ので楽しみです。」
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)  
Answer 1: 今年が (3rd position)

Correct Order  
4 → 2 → 1 → 3  
📌 夏休みを → 過ごすのは → 今年が → 初めて

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown  
④ 夏休みを — object marker; “summer vacation” as the object of 過ごす  
② 過ごすのは — verb + nominaliser の + topic は; “spending (it)” becomes the topic  
① 今年が — “this year” marked by が; subject/focus before 初めて (“this year is the first time”)  
③ 初めて — “for the first time”; links to ので as 初めてなので to give the reason

Reconstructed Sentence  
いいえ、今年は帰りません。日本で夏休みを過ごすのは今年が初めてなので楽しみです。  
→ No, I won’t go back this year. It’s my first time spending summer vacation in Japan, so I’m looking forward to it.

Why This Order Works  
1. 夏休みを is the object of 過ごす.  
2. 過ごすのは nominalises and topics the activity.  
3. 今年が pairs naturally with 初めて: “this year is the first time.”  
4. 初めて + ので → 初めてなので gives the reason for 楽しみです.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 (★자리=세 번째 「今年が」). 올바른 순서: 夏休みを→過ごすのは→今年が→初めて.
포인트: 「Vるのは…が初めて」(V하는 것은 …가 처음). 「日本で夏休みを過ごすのは今年が初めて」(일본에서 여름방학을 보내는 것은 올해가 처음).

questions.stem: さくら大学の周りには、レストランや喫茶店などの____　____　★　____ある。
questions.expl:
Answer (★ Position)  
Answer 4: 本屋や美容院なども (★ is 3rd position)

Correct Order  
2 → 1 → 4 → 3  
📌 飲食店を → 中心に → 本屋や美容院なども → いろいろな店が

Chunk-by-Chunk Breakdown  
② 飲食店を — object marker を; attaches to the preceding phrase レストランや喫茶店などの to form “(the) eateries such as restaurants and cafés”  
① 中心に — “centered on; mainly”; N を中心に = “mainly around N”  
④ 本屋や美容院なども — “bookstores and beauty salons, etc., too”; adds other examples with も “also”  
③ いろいろな店が — subject phrase “various shops”; pairs with ある to state existence

Reconstructed Sentence  
さくら大学の周りには、レストランや喫茶店などの飲食店を中心に、本屋や美容院なども、いろいろな店がある。  
→ Around Sakura University, mainly eateries like restaurants and cafés, and also various other shops such as bookstores and beauty salons, can be found.

Why This Order Works  
- 飲食店を + 中心に is a fixed pattern meaning “centered on/mainly.”  
- 本屋や美容院なども adds additional categories with も “also.”  
- いろいろな店が must come right before ある to form “there are various shops.”  
- The ★ chunk (4) naturally sits in the 3rd slot between the modifier phrase and the subject.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 (★자리=세 번째 「本屋や美容院なども」). 올바른 순서: 飲食店を→中心に→本屋や美容院なども→いろいろな店が.
포인트: 「Nを中心に」(N을 중심으로)+「など+も」(~도). 「飲食店を中心に本屋や美容院などもいろいろな店がある」(음식점을 중심으로 서점·미용실 등 여러 가게가 있다).

questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 2: 見学した is correct. It means "visited" or "went on a tour."

**Question Translation**  
What I visited was a factory of an ice cream company.

**Explanation**  
The correct answer, 見学した, is in the past tense, which matches the context of the passage where the speaker describes a past event — their visit to an ice cream factory. The use of past tense is appropriate as it aligns with the narrative that the factory visit happened last month.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: 見学する is in the present or future tense, meaning "to visit" or "to tour." It doesn't fit the retrospective context where the speaker describes an event that has already occurred.
- Answer 3: 見学している means "is visiting" or "is in the process of touring," which indicates a present continuous action. This is incorrect as the passage describes a past visit.
- Answer 4: 見学してある implies a state where a visiting action has been completed for a certain purpose, but it doesn't fit the simple past tense needed to describe the visit in the narrative context.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
Recognizing the correct tense is critical in Japanese to accurately convey the timing of actions. In this case, the past tense "見学した" exactly describes an experience that has concluded, which is a common structure in recounting past events or experiences.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 「見学した」 — 견학했다(과거).
근거: 앞에 「先月ついに行くことができました」(지난달 마침내 갔다)라는 과거 경험이 나오므로, 「見学したのはアイスクリームの工場です」(견학한 곳은 아이스크림 공장)가 자연스럽다.
오답: 1 見学する(현재·미래), 3 見学している(진행), 4 見学してある는 시제·태가 안 맞는다.

questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 1: でも is correct. It means "however" or "but."

**Question Translation**  
Even so, I did not understand why they allow visits for free.

**Explanation**  
The passage is describing the writer's experience at a factory visit that was free of charge. The sentence following the experience review states that, despite the positive visit, the writer did not understand why it was free. "でも" (demo) is used here to indicate a contrast between the enjoyable visit and their confusion about the lack of a fee, making it the correct conjunction to show this shift in perspective.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 2: また (mata) means "again" or "also." It does not introduce a contrast, so it would not fit in a sentence indicating a change of thought or understanding.  
- Answer 3: すると (suruto) means "and then” or “thereupon,” often used to indicate a sequence of events. It does not fit here because the sentence is contrasting ideas rather than describing a sequence.  
- Answer 4: たとえば (tatoeba) means "for example." This word is used to introduce examples, which do not align with the subsequent content indicating contrast.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The conjunction "でも" is commonly used to introduce a contrasting idea, similar to “however” or “but” in English. This is useful when you wish to juxtapose two related yet contrasting thoughts or situations you see often in experiences and reports. This is important for understanding how ideas are linked or contrasted in larger texts.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 「でも」 — 그래도·하지만(역접).
근거: 앞은 「工場見学は本当に楽しかった」(즐거웠다), 뒤는 「どうして無料でさせてくれるのかわかりませんでした」(왜 무료인지 몰랐다)로 의문 제시. 즐거움→의문의 전환은 역접.
오답: 2 また(또), 3 すると(그러자), 4 たとえば(예를 들면) — 흐름이 맞지 않는다.

questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 4: そこ is correct. It refers to "there," implying the location mentioned in the prior context.

**Question Translation**  
According to a newspaper survey, the majority of people who visited the factory came to like products made "there" and also formed a better image of the company.

**Explanation**  
The pronoun そこ (soko) is used for referring to a location or concept previously mentioned, but not in the immediate proximity of the speaker. In this passage, そこ appropriately refers back to the factory where the products are made, indicating the connection between the location and the resulting positive impression.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: これ (kore) means "this" and is typically used to refer to something close to the speaker. It doesn't fit since the context is referring to the factory described earlier.  
- Answer 2: それ (sore) means "that" and is used for something mentioned in the conversation but is not far in terms of understanding. While similar to そこ, それ is not suitable for specifying a location.  
- Answer 3: ここ (koko) means "here" and refers to a location near the speaker. It doesn't fit because the sentence refers to a previously mentioned location (the factory) rather than a current location.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
Understanding demonstrative pronouns like これ, それ, ここ, and そこ is crucial as they are often used in Japanese to refer back to ideas or locations within a conversation or text. In this context, そこ is specific to indicating a place that is mentioned but not present where the speaker currently is.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 「そこ」 — 거기(앞서 언급한 장소를 가리킴).
근거: 앞 문맥의 「工場」(공장)을 받아 「そこで作られている商品」(거기서 만들어지는 상품)이라고 가리킨다.
오답: 1 これ·2 それ는 사물 지시, 3 ここ는 화자 가까이. 장소+제3자적 거리는 「そこ」.

opts: 行ってみてほしいです
行ってみたがっています
行ってみるつもりです
行ってみるといいです
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 3: 行ってみるつもりです is correct. It means "I intend to go and try going to various factories."

**Question Translation**  
I am looking for factories to visit next. While I am in Japan, I intend to try visiting various factories.

**Explanation**  
The sentence discusses future plans regarding visiting other factories during the speaker's time in Japan. 行ってみるつもりです (いってみる つもりです) indicates a clear intention or plan, fitting the context of expressing a decision to continue this activity.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: 行ってみてほしいです means "I want someone to try going." This suggests a desire for someone else to visit, not the speaker's intention.
- Answer 2: 行ってみたがっています means "Someone wants to try going." The phrasing suggests a third person's desire, inconsistent with the self-directed decision context.
- Answer 4: 行ってみるといいです means "It would be good to try going." This suggests a recommendation or advice, which doesn’t align with the expression of a personal intention or plan.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
つもり (つもり) is a noun used with verbs to express one's intention or plan to do something. Recognizing expressions that convey intentions versus suggestions or desires is key in discerning the intended meaning in sentences involving plans or decisions.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 「行ってみるつもりです」 — 가 볼 생각입니다.
근거: 앞 문장 「次に見学に行く工場を探しています」(다음 견학할 공장을 찾고 있다)에서 본인의 의지·계획을 드러내므로 「つもり」(작정)가 맞다.
오답: 1 ほしい(상대에게 바람), 2 たがる(제3자의 욕망), 4 といい(권유) — 본인의 계획 표현이 아니다.

questions.stem: 「私」はどうして目標の500字を覚えることができたと考えているか。
opts: 「私」にとって簡単な目標だったから。
クラス全員で話し合って、同じ目標に決めたから。
簡単にあきらめないようにしようと、心の中で決めていたから。
決めた目標が、自分にもほかの人にも見えるようにしてあったから。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 4: 決めた目標が、自分にもほかの人にも見えるようにしてあったから. It means "because the set goal was visible to both myself and others."

**Question Translation**  
Why does "I" think they were able to memorize 500 kanji characters?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes a situation where the "I" decided to memorize 500 kanji characters, which was challenging. However, by writing the goal on a piece of paper and putting it in a classroom where both "I" and classmates could see it, "I" was reminded of the goal regularly and couldn't easily give up. Thus, the visibility of the goal both personally and socially (to others in the class) played a crucial role in maintaining accountability and motivation to achieve it. This matches exactly with Answer 4, which emphasizes visibility to oneself and others as a key factor.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: 「私」にとって簡単な目標だったから. This suggests the goal was easy for "I," but the passage clearly states the goal of learning 500 kanji was challenging.
- Answer 2: クラス全員で話し合って、同じ目標に決めたから. This implies a collective decision on the same goal, but the passage specifies individualized goals written and posted, not a shared goal among the class.
- Answer 3: 簡単にあきらめないようにしようと、心の中で決めていたから. This suggests "I" had an internal decision not to give up. While internal determination is essential, the passage emphasizes the external and visible nature of the goal as crucial, as stated in Answer 4.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The passage illustrates practical motivation strategies, emphasizing the effectiveness of external reinforcement (visual reminders) over internal resolutions alone. Common phrases include 目標 (もくひょう, "goal") and 覚える (おぼえる, "to memorize"). Recognizing how visibility can reinforce personal commitments is key in understanding the motivation described in the passage.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 정한 목표가 자신과 다른 사람에게 보이도록 했기 때문.
근거: 「自分で書いた目標が目に入るし、クラスのみんなも知っているから、簡単にはやめられない」(자기가 쓴 목표가 눈에 들어오고 반 친구들도 알아서 쉽게 그만둘 수 없었다).
오답: 1 '쉬운 목표'는 본문과 반대(「大変だった」), 2 '의논'은 없음, 3 '마음속'은 효과 없는 방법으로 부정됨.

questions.stem: 旅行会社がこのメールで最も言いたい事は何か。
opts: 旅行の予約内容を確認したら、連絡してください。
旅行代金を出発日の1週間前までに振り込んでください。
旅行のキャンセル料を1週間以内に支払ってください。
旅行について何かわからないことがあれば質問をしてください。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 2: 旅行代金を出発日の1週間前までに振り込んでください。 It means "Please transfer the travel fee one week before the departure date."

**Question Translation**  
What is the main thing that the travel agency wants to convey in this email?

**Explanation**  
The primary purpose of the email is to thank Mizukawa-san for applying for the tour and to remind them to transfer the travel fee by one week before the departure date. This information is emphasized right after the initial "thank you" for the application, indicating its importance. Therefore, the focus of the email is on the payment deadline, which aligns directly with Answer 2.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- **Answer 1**: Saying "Please contact us after confirming the travel reservation details" is incorrect because the email's focus is not on asking for a confirmation response but rather on the payment deadline.
- **Answer 3**: "Please pay the cancellation fee within one week" is incorrect because the email doesn't ask Mizukawa-san to pay any cancellation fees; it explains the cancellation policy instead.
- **Answer 4**: While the email mentions contacting the agency with questions, this is a side note and not the main point of the email. The main purpose is to instruct the customer to pay by a specific date.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The phrase "お振り込みください" (おふりこみください) is a polite way to request someone to transfer money, commonly used in business communications to denote a payment transaction. Recognizing polite verb forms and payment instructions is crucial in understanding formal business interactions in Japanese.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 출발 1주일 전까지 여행 대금을 입금해 주세요.
근거: 메일 본문 「ご出発日の1週間前までに旅行代金をお振り込みください」가 가장 강조된 안내. 예약 확인은 보조 안내.
오답: 1 '연락'은 보조, 3 '취소료'는 조건부 정보, 4 '질문'은 부가 안내.

questions.stem: 意外なサービスとあるが一人暮らしのお年寄りとその家族に対するどのようなサービスか。
opts: 家族の指定した時間に、お年寄りに荷物を配達する。
お年寄りを訪問し、その様子を家族にEメールで連絡する。
家族からのEメールを郵便局員が受け取り、印刷してお年寄りに渡す。
ふだんなかなか会えない家族のところに、お年寄りを連れて行く。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 2: お年寄りを訪問し、その様子を家族にEメールで連絡する is correct. It means "Visit the elderly person and report their condition to the family via email."

**Question Translation**  
The question asks: An unexpected service is mentioned, but what kind of service is it for elderly people living alone and their families?

**Explanation**  
The passage discusses a unique service provided by the post office, where a postal worker visits an elderly person living alone to check on them and then reports their condition to the family via email. This is specifically designed to provide peace of mind to the family who might not be able to visit frequently. Answer 2 directly describes this service, making it an accurate reflection of the passage.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: 家族の指定した時間に、お年寄りに荷物を配達する means "Deliver packages to the elderly at a time specified by the family." This does not mention checking on the elderly person’s wellbeing or contacting the family, so it doesn’t fit the description of the service in the passage.
- Answer 3: 家族からのEメールを郵便局員が受け取り、印刷してお年寄りに渡す means "The postal worker receives an email from the family, prints it, and gives it to the elderly person." This describes a communication in the opposite direction—from family to the elderly—which is not mentioned in the passage.
- Answer 4: ふだんなかなか会えない家族のところに、お年寄りを連れて行く means "Take the elderly to the family who they usually cannot meet." This implies a transportation service, which is not what the passage is about. The passage highlights a check-in service rather than physically moving the elderly person.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- 意外 (いがい) means "unexpected" or "surprising." It is used to describe something that does not align with common expectations.
- The use of Eメール (email) exemplifies how modern technology is integrated into traditional services to enhance communication and provide reassurance. Recognizing the context and function of services is essential in understanding the application of such vocabulary in real-world scenarios.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 어르신을 방문해 그 모습을 가족에게 이메일로 알리는 서비스.
근거: 「郵便局員がお年寄りの家を訪問する。そのときのお年寄りの様子をEメールで家族に教えてくれる」(우체국 직원이 댁을 방문, 모습을 가족에게 이메일로 알림).
오답: 1 '시간 지정 배달'은 본문과 다름, 3 '가족→어르신' 방향이 반대, 4 '데려가다'는 본문에 없음.

questions.stem: ナムさんがこのメモで頼まれていることは何か。
opts: 資料を60部準備すること、教室が変わったことを全員にメールで知らせること。
準備する資料を20部増やすことと、304のドアにはる紙を作っておくこと。
教室の予約を304から201を変えることと、304のドアに紙をはること。
教室が変わったことを全員にメールで知らせることと、304のドアにはる紙を作っておくこと。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 2 is correct. It means "to increase the number of prepared materials by 20 copies and to prepare the paper to be posted on the door of room 304."

**Question Translation**  
What is Mr. Nam being asked to do in this memo?

**Explanation**  
The passage is a memo instructing Mr. Nam on actions related to the upcoming "grammar study group." Firstly, it mentions an increase in expected participants, requesting an additional 20 sets of materials, which suggests a total of 60 attendees. Secondly, it notes that the room booking was changed from room 304 to 201. Despite the change being informed via email, it suggests creating a notice to inform attendees on the day by posting a paper on room 304's door. Answer 2 accurately reflects these tasks: increasing the materials by 20 sets and making a sign for room 304.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: Incorrect because it incorrectly states preparing 60 sets of materials initially, which is a misunderstanding. It involves preparing only 20 more, not 60 total.
- Answer 3: Incorrect because the task of changing the room reservation is already done and not Mr. Nam's responsibility. This option ignores the key task of preparing 20 additional copies of materials.
- Answer 4: Incorrect because it repeats the task of sending email notifications, which Mr. Nam is not instructed to do; the email notifications are already taken care of by the sender. 

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The term "部" (ぶ) refers to a 'copy' or 'set,' commonly used in contexts like preparing or requesting a specific number of documents or materials. The verb phrase "作っておいてください" implies preparing something in advance, addressing future needs, which in this context pertains to preparing the door notice. Recognizing verbs indicating preparatory actions is crucial for accurately interpreting tasks and instructions in Japanese.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 2번 — 자료를 20부 더 준비하는 것과 304문에 붙일 종이를 만들어 두는 것.
근거: 「資料をあと20部お願いします」(자료 20부 추가)+「304のドアに…紙をはったほうがいい…作っておいてください」(304문에 붙일 종이 작성 부탁).
오답: 1 '60부 준비'는 결과 인원수일 뿐 부탁 자체가 아님. 3 '교실 예약 변경'은 이미 사무실이 함. 4 '전원 이메일 통지'는 작성자가 직접 함.

questions.stem: ①この水筒とあるが、どのような水筒か。
opts: 飲み物温度が変わりにくい、直接口をつけて飲む水筒。
飲み物の温度が変わりにくい、コップが付いている水筒。
熱い飲み物を冷ましやすい、直接口をつけて飲む水筒
熱い飲み物を冷ましやすい、コップがついている水筒
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 1: 飲み物温度が変わりにくい、直接口をつけて飲む水筒 is correct. It means "a bottle that keeps the drink’s temperature stable and allows you to drink directly from it."

**Question Translation**  
There is a mention of "this water bottle," but what kind of water bottle is it?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes the specific features of the water bottle the person bought. They chose a bottle that allows direct drinking without using a cup, which prevents the temperature of the beverage from changing significantly over time. This characteristic perfectly matches Answer 1: 飲み物温度が変わりにくい (the temperature of the drink does not change easily) and 直接口をつけて飲む (drink directly from it). Hence, this option accurately reflects the description provided in the passage about the water bottle.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 2: 飲み物の温度が変わりにくい、コップが付いている水筒. This mentions a water bottle that comes with a cup, which contradicts the passage where it states choosing a bottle because it didn’t have a cup.
- Answer 3: 熱い飲み物を冷ましやすい、直接口をつけて飲む水筒. Focuses on the aspect of cooling hot drinks, which isn’t aligned with the passage. The issue arose because the bottle was suitable for room-temperature drinks, not for cooling hot drinks directly.
- Answer 4: 熱い飲み物を冷ましやすい、コップがついている水筒. Suggests a bottle that helps cool hot drinks using a cup, which goes against the passage's earlier detail illustrating the issue faced due to the initial design, which excluded a cup.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- **直接口をつけて飲む**: This phrase means "to drink directly by applying one's mouth," highlighting that the bottle doesn’t require pouring into a cup.
- Understanding the function and description of items in context descriptions can often depend heavily on the adjectives and specific phrases pointing out their features.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 — 음료 온도가 잘 변하지 않고, 직접 입을 대고 마시는 물통.
근거: 「コップが付いていない、直接口をつけて飲む水筒で、飲み物の温度が長時間変わらない物」(컵이 없고 직접 입을 대 마시는, 음료 온도가 오래 안 변하는 물통).
오답: 2 '컵 부착'은 사지 않은 다른 종류, 3·4 '식히기 쉽다'는 보온성과 반대.

questions.stem: ②困ったこととあるが、どのようなことか。
opts: 熱い飲み物を外で飲む機会が少なくなったこと。
熱い飲み物を入れら、水筒が壊れてしまったこと。
熱いもの飲み物が水筒から直接飲めなかったこと。
熱い飲み物がすぐに冷めてしまったこと。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 3: 熱いもの飲み物が水筒から直接飲めなかったこと is correct. It means "I couldn't drink hot beverages directly from the thermos."

**Question Translation**  
What does the phrase "困ったこと" (problem/issue) refer to in the passage?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes the issue that arose when the narrator first used a thermos without a cup to drink hot beverages. During summer, the thermos was convenient for keeping drinks cold, but in winter, when hot drinks were needed, the direct drinking design became a problem because the drinks were too hot to drink directly. This led the narrator to buy a thermos with a cup to cool the hot drinks. Answer 3 accurately reflects the problem described: they couldn't directly drink hot beverages from the thermos.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: 熱い飲み物を外で飲む機会が少なくなったこと ("Having fewer opportunities to drink hot beverages outside") is incorrect. The issue was not about the frequency of drinking hot beverages, but about the inability to drink them directly due to high temperature.
- Answer 2: 熱い飲み物を入れら、水筒が壊れてしまったこと ("The thermos broke when hot drinks were put in") is incorrect. The passage does not mention any damage to the thermos; rather, it focuses on the difficulty of drinking directly due to heat.
- Answer 4: 熱い飲み物がすぐに冷めてしまったこと ("The hot beverage cooled down too quickly") is incorrect. The passage highlights the problem of drinks being too hot to drink from the thermos, not about the drinks cooling too fast.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- The phrase "困ったこと" (こまったこと) means "problem" or "predicament." Understanding the nature of the problem described in the passage is crucial to selecting the correct answer.
- The structure in "熱すぎて、直接は飲めなかった" (あつすぎて、ちょくせつはのめなかった) involves the "すぎる" grammar pattern which implies "too much," highlighting that the beverages were excessively hot to drink directly.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 — 뜨거운 음료를 물통에서 직접 마실 수 없었던 일.
근거: 「熱すぎて、直接は飲めなかった」(너무 뜨거워서 직접은 마실 수 없었다).
오답: 1 '기회 감소'는 본문에 없음, 2 '물통이 깨졌다'는 없음, 4 '바로 식었다'는 보온 기능과 모순.

questions.stem: ③最初に考えていたよりはお金がかかってしまったとあるが、それはなぜか。
opts: ペットボトルを買うことが増えたから
とても高い水筒を買うことになったから。
水筒とは別にコップも買うことになったから。
水筒を2本も買うことになったから
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 4: 水筒を2本も買うことになったから is correct. It means "because I ended up buying two flasks."

**Question Translation**  
The question asks: "In reference to the statement 'it cost more than I first thought,' why is that?"

**Explanation**  
In the passage, the author initially purchased one type of flask thinking it would be sufficient, but due to the inconvenience of drinking hot beverages, they ended up purchasing a second flask with a cup. This led to spending more money than initially planned. The key sentence explaining this is: "最初は1本あれば十分だと思っていたが、結局、コップ付きの水筒も買った," indicating that buying the second flask resulted in extra expenses.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: ペットボトルを買うことが増えたから ("because the purchase of PET bottles increased") is incorrect. The passage states that buying PET bottles has stopped, so this option does not fit.
- Answer 2: とても高い水筒を買うことになったから ("because I ended up buying a very expensive flask") is incorrect. The passage mentions that neither flask was particularly expensive.
- Answer 3: 水筒とは別にコップも買うことになったから ("because I ended up buying a cup in addition to the flask") is incorrect. The passage does not indicate the purchase of a separate cup; it mentions buying a flask with an attached cup.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The phrase "最初に考えていたよりはお金がかかってしまった" involves a comparison ("more than initially thought") indicating spending going beyond expectations. The verb "かかる" (kakaru) here is used in the context of costing money. Understanding comparative conjunctions and how monetary contexts are expressed in Japanese is crucial for such questions.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 물통을 두 개나 사게 되었기 때문.
근거: 「結局、コップ付きの水筒も買った」(결국 컵 부착 물통도 샀다)→총 두 개 구매로 예상 외 지출.
오답: 1 페트병 구매는 줄었다(「ペットボトルも買わなくなった」), 2 '비싼 물통'은 「それほど高くはなかった」와 모순, 3 '컵만' 따로는 본문과 다름.
questions.stem_u: 最初に考えていたよりはお金がかかってしまった

questions.stem: ①この問題とあるが、何か
opts: 日本にカラスが多すぎること。
日本にカラスを嫌がる人が多いこと。
カラスが人に向かって飛んできたり、食物を取ったりして、危険で困ること。
「私」の町の公園に、カラスがたくさん集まってきて、うるさくて怖いこと。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 4: 「私」の町の公園に、カラスがたくさん集まってきて、うるさくて怖いこと is correct. It means "In my town's park, many crows gather, making it noisy and frightening."

**Question Translation**  
It is mentioned as "this problem," but what does it refer to?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes a specific problem where crows gather in large numbers in the park of "my" town, resulting in a noisy and somewhat fear-inducing situation for the residents. The phrase "この問題" (this problem) directly refers to this situation as it is described with emphasis on the inconvenience it causes ("声がうるさいし、数が非常に多いので怖さを感じることもあります"), and there is a direct mention of the town's people being troubled by it. This aligns perfectly with Answer 4, making it the correct choice.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: 日本にカラスが多すぎること (The fact that there are too many crows in Japan) is too broad and generalized. The passage focuses specifically on the problem within "my" town, not nationwide in Japan.
- Answer 2: 日本にカラスを嫌がる人が多いこと (The fact that many people in Japan dislike crows) is irrelevant to the specific issue discussed in the passage, which focuses on a specific locale rather than widespread human dislike.
- Answer 3: カラスが人に向かって飛んできたり、食物を取ったりして、危険で困ること (The danger and trouble caused by crows flying toward people and taking food) is mentioned in the passage but under a different context. It's not specifically highlighted as "the problem" when compared to the noise and intimidation caused by large gatherings in the park.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- "この問題" (このもんだい): "This problem" is a demonstrative noun phrase that directly connects a prior issue discussed in the text, useful for referring back to specific points. Understanding such connections is crucial in comprehension sections of the JLPT.
- "嫌がる" (いやがる): "To dislike" is an emotional verb that can often indicate discomfort or dissatisfaction, often paired with casual or habitual actions to describe general feelings.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 내 동네 공원에 까마귀가 많이 모여 시끄럽고 무서운 것.
근거: 「私の町では…公園の木で寝るために集まってきます。声がうるさいし、数が非常に多いので怖さを感じる…町の人たちはとても困っています。①この問題」(우리 동네 공원에 모여 시끄럽고 무섭다는 것이 바로 ①이 문제).
오답: 1·2는 일본 일반론, 3은 까마귀의 습성 설명일 뿐 '내 동네'의 구체 문제는 아니다.

questions.stem: どのような②実験か
opts: カラスを「集まっても問題のない場所」でよく眠らせて、静かにさせる実験。
カラスが「いてほしくない場所」にいても、鳴き声は周りに聞こえないようにする実験。
カラスの苦手な食べ物を利用して、「いてほしくない場所」に来ないようにする実験。
カラスの鳴き方の違いを利用して、「東でも問題のない場所」に行かせる実験。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 4: カラスの鳴き方の違いを利用して、「集まっても問題のない場所」に行かせる実験 is correct. It means "an experiment that uses the differences in the way crows call to make them go to a place where it's not a problem for them to gather."

**Question Translation**  
What kind of experiment is it?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes an experiment conducted in another town. Specialists helped in distinguishing between the calls of crows when they feel safe and when they perceive danger. This was used to move crows from places where they are not wanted to places where their presence would not be an issue. Answer 4 accurately states this by mentioning the utilization of the crows' call differences to send them to a "problem-free" location.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: カラスを「集まっても問題のない場所」でよく眠らせて、静かにさせる実験. This suggests letting crows sleep quietly in a problem-free place. The passage focuses on moving crows, not on letting them sleep.
- Answer 2: カラスが「いてほしくない場所」にいても、鳴き声は周りに聞こえないようにする実験. This is about making their calls inaudible in unwanted places, which doesn't align with the idea of moving the crows.
- Answer 3: カラスの苦手な食べ物を利用して、「いてほしくない場所」に来ないようにする実験. This suggests using food to repel crows, which is not mentioned in the passage. The text centers on using the difference in crow calls.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- **実験 (じっけん)**: This means "experiment." Recognizing specialized terms is crucial in comprehension sections to understand the subject matter.
- Understanding the way context shapes the usage of expressions like "集まっても問題のない場所" (場所: ばしょ, place) is important for recognizing how locations and conditions affect the actions of subjects like crows in the passage.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 4번 — 까마귀의 울음소리 차이를 이용해 '모여도 문제없는 장소'로 가게 하는 실험.
근거: 「安全な状態にいるときと危険を感じたときのカラスの鳴き方の違いを利用して、『いてほしくない場所』から『集まっても問題のない場所』にカラスを移動させる」.
오답: 1 '재우다'는 본문에 없음, 2 '울음소리 차단'은 본문과 다름, 3 '음식 이용'은 언급 없음.

questions.stem: 「私」の町のカラスの問題について、「私」はどう考えているか
opts: 「私」の町で成功した方法を、これからも続けていきたい。
「私」の町でも、別の町で成功した方法を使って解決してほしい。
別の町で成功した方法とは違う、「私」の町に合った新しい方法が必要だ。
別の町で成功した方法は「私」の町では使えないから、解決はあきらめよう。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 3: 別の町で成功した方法とは違う、「私」の町に合った新しい方法が必要だ。 It means that a new method suitable to my town, different from the successful method used in another town, is needed.

**Question Translation**  
What does "I" think about the crow problem in "my" town?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes a crow problem in the speaker's town and explains that a certain method was successfully used in another town to manage crows. However, due to the lack of suitable alternative locations in this person's town, the same method cannot be applied. The speaker hopes for a solution that fits the particular conditions of their town. This supports answer 3, as it highlights the need for a new method tailored specifically for their location.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: Claims the method was successful in the speaker's town, which is incorrect; the method was successful elsewhere, and cannot continue here due to different conditions.
- Answer 2: Suggests using the successful method from another town in their town. The passage clearly states that the successful method cannot be used because there's no suitable area for the crows to relocate.
- Answer 4: Implies giving up on solving the problem completely, which contradicts the speaker's expressed desire to find a suitable solution for their town.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- 解決方法 (かいけつほうほう): "Solution method"; a key phrase that indicates the search for an effective way to solve a specific problem.
- 行けそうにありません (つかえそうにありません): A grammatical structure indicating that something doesn’t seem possible or feasible. It reflects the speaker’s reasoning regarding the inapplicability of the existing solution. Recognizing this nuance is crucial in understanding the passage’s context.
questions.expl_ko:

근거: 「私の町には大きな森など、『集まっても問題のない場所』はありません。…同じ方法は使えそうにありません。私の町に合った解決方法が見つかればいい」.

questions.stem: ①「あきらめ始めていた」とあるが、何をあきらめ始めていたのか。
opts: 両親に今まで違うプレゼントを送ること。
両親にいつもと同じプレゼントを送ること。
両親に結婚記念日までにプレゼントを送ること。
両親に欲しいものを決めてもらうこと。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 1: 両親に今まで違うプレゼントを送ること is correct. It means "sending different presents to my parents so far."

**Question Translation**  
The question: What was being given up as stated with "あきらめ始めていた"?
The passage indicates: "I was starting to give up on always sending my parents different presents."

**Explanation**  
The passage discusses how the speaker has been sending different presents to their parents every year for 20 years. However, they are running out of ideas and considered resigning themselves to giving something they have given before — a change from their usual approach of always presenting something different and unique. This indicates the speaker was giving up on the notion of sending different presents each year.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 2: 両親にいつもと同じプレゼントを送ること ("sending the same present as always to my parents") is incorrect because the passage specifies the speaker is used to sending different presents, not the same one each year.
  
- Answer 3: 両親に結婚記念日までにプレゼントを送ること ("sending the present by the anniversary") doesn't apply, as the focus is on the variety of presents given, not the timing.

- Answer 4: 両親に欲しいものを決めてもらうこと ("letting the parents decide what they want") is not mentioned in the passage. The speaker contemplates lack of ideas and potential alternatives, but there is no mention of asking the parents for their preference.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The expression "あきらめ始めていた" (akirame hajimeteita) means "was starting to give up." This indicates an ongoing process where the speaker initially intended to keep up a tradition but gradually shifted towards resignation due to difficulty maintaining that standard over time.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 1번 — 부모에게 지금까지와 다른 선물을 보내는 것.
근거: 「もう20年以上続けているので、だんだんアイディアがなくなってきた。今年は、前にあげたことがあるものでもしかたない」(20년 이상 매번 다른 선물→아이디어 고갈→이전 것이라도 어쩔 수 없다고 단념).
오답: 2 '같은 선물'은 본문과 반대, 3 '기념일까지'는 시기가 아닌 종류 문제, 4 '본인에게 결정'은 본문에 없음.

questions.stem: 「私」が②説明の紙を読んで知ったことは、どんなことか。
opts: ろうそくの火が、不規則なゆれ方をすること。
ろうそくの火が、人の気持ちを落ち着かせること。
ろうそくの火が、嫌なにおいを消すこと。
ろうそくの火が、料理をおいしく見せること。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 4: ろうそくの火が、料理をおいしく見せること is correct. It means "The candle's flame makes food look more delicious."

**Question Translation**  
What did "I" learn by reading the explanation paper?

**Explanation**  
The sentence from the passage that informs the correct answer states: "ろうそくの火、嫌なにおいを消す効果があることは知っていたが、料理をおいしく見せる効果があることは初めて知った." This translates to "I knew that the candle's flame has the effect of removing bad odors, but I learned for the first time that it has the effect of making food look more delicious." This indicates that the new piece of information learned was about enhancing the appearance of the food.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- **Answer 1**: ろうそくの火が、不規則なゆれ方をすること — This option mentions the irregular flickering of the candle's flame, which is described earlier in the passage but not as new information learned from the explanation paper.
- **Answer 2**: ろうそくの火が、人の気持ちを落ち着かせること — This refers to the candle's calming effect, which the speaker experiences firsthand, but it is not specified as new information from the paper.
- **Answer 3**: ろうそくの火が、嫌なにおいを消すこと — The passage explicitly states that the speaker already knew about this odor-removing effect of the candle's flame, so it wasn't something newly learned from the explanation sheet.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- **効果 (こうか)**: "Effect" or "efficacy." This is used to describe the various effects the candle has, such as removing odors and enhancing the appearance of food.
- The distinction between known information and newly learned details is crucial for selecting the correct option, as it focuses on what was new knowledge gained from the explanation paper.
questions.expl_ko:

근거: 「ろうそくの火、嫌なにおいを消す効果があることは知っていたが、料理をおいしく見せる効果があることは初めて知った」(냄새 제거는 알았지만 음식 효과는 처음 앎).
오답: 1 '불꽃이 흔들림'은 직접 본 것, 2 '마음 차분'도 직접 체험, 3 '냄새 제거'는 이미 알고 있던 것.

questions.stem: ③ろうそくはやっぱりやめたほうがいいと思ったとあるが、なぜか。
opts: 前に両親にろうそくをあげたことを思い出したから。
結婚記念日にろうそくあげるのは変だと思ったから。
ろうそくの火は危険かもしれないと心配になったから。
ろうそくより新しいペットをあげた方が喜ばれると思ったから。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 3: ろうそくの火は危険かもしれないと心配になったから. This means "Because I became worried that the candle flame might be dangerous."

**Question Translation**  
Why do they think, "I should probably give up on the candles"?

**Explanation**  
In the passage, the narrator initially considers giving candles as a present because they believe it might enhance their parents' anniversary dinner experience. However, while thinking it over, they start to worry about the safety of the candle flame. The passage mentions concerns about leaving the candle unattended after a meal and the fact that their parents have a cat, which introduces a risk of an accident. This concern about the potential danger of the open flame is why option 3 is the correct answer.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: "前に両親にろうそくをあげたことを思い出したから" implies the narrator remembered previously giving candles as a gift. The passage clearly states they have not given candles before.
- Answer 2: "結婚記念日にろうそくあげるのは変だと思ったから" suggests thinking it's weird to give candles for an anniversary. However, the text does not state or imply this.
- Answer 4: "ろうそくより新しいペットをあげた方が喜ばれると思ったから" suggests a preference for giving a pet over candles. This is not mentioned in the passage.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
The verb phrase "やめたほうがいい" is used to indicate that not doing something would be better. This is essential for understanding the narrator's decision-making process in the context of safety concerns regarding candles. Also, "やっぱり" indicates a reconsideration or confirmation of a decision after deliberation, reflecting the change in the narrator’s thought process.
questions.expl_ko:
정답: 3번 — 양초 불이 위험할지 모른다고 걱정됐기 때문.
근거: 「食事のあとで消し忘れることもあるかもしれない。それに、両親の家では猫を飼っているので、何が起こるかわからない」(끄는 걸 잊거나 고양이 때문에 사고 우려).
오답: 1 '이전에 준 적'은 본문과 반대, 2 '이상하다'는 이유는 언급 없음, 4 '펫 선물'은 무관.
questions.stem_u: ろうそくはやっぱりやめたほうがいいと思った

questions.stem: 両親へのプレゼントについて、「私」は今、どのように考えているか。
opts: 友達にもらったろうそくがよかったから、同じろうそくが売られているか調べよう。
安全に使える「電気ろうそく」を見つけたから、さっそく両親に送ろう。
「電気ろうそく」にしようと思うが、もう少し調べてからどれにするか決めよう。
「電気ろうそく」をあげるのはやめて、ろうそくについてもう一度調べてみよう。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 3: 「電気ろうそく」にしようと思うが、もう少し調べてからどれにするか決めよう. It means "I think I'll go with the 'electric candle', but I'll decide which one after doing a bit more research."

**Question Translation**  
What is the speaker currently thinking about the gift for their parents?

**Explanation**  
The passage describes the speaker's dilemma about yearly gifts for their parents' wedding anniversary. Initially concerned about repeating past gifts, the speaker considers a new candle, but then worries about safety issues due to the presence of cats in their parents' home. They find an "electric candle" as a safer alternative. Despite discovering this option, the speaker intends to research further before making a final decision. This is clearly articulated in the passage: "もう少し調べてみようと思う," indicating their plan to investigate more.

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
- Answer 1: Suggests checking if the same candle received from a friend is sold elsewhere. This is incorrect because the speaker's concern shifts from the physical candle due to safety reasons to an electric one, reflecting their intention to find alternatives rather than the same type of candle.
- Answer 2: Implies that the speaker will immediately send the "electric candle" to their parents. This is incorrect because the passage states that they plan to investigate more options, indicating that an immediate decision has not been made.
- Answer 4: Suggests the speaker will not give the "electric candle" and instead research candles again. This contradicts the passage where the speaker is specifically interested in exploring types of "electric candles" as potential gifts.

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- **諦め始めていた (あきらめはじめていた)**: A compound verb form indicating the beginning of giving up ("start giving up").
- **やっぱり (やっぱり)**: Means "after all" or "as expected," used here to reflect a reconsideration or reaffirmation of an earlier thought.
- **調べてみよう (しらべてみよう)**: A volitional form suggesting an intention to look into or investigate further, indicating plans are not finalized.
questions.expl_ko:

근거: 위험을 우려해 일반 양초를 포기하고 전기양초로 마음이 기울되, 종류·안전성 확인 후 최종 결정하려는 흐름이 결말에 제시된다.
오답: 1 '같은 양초 구매'는 위험 우려로 포기, 2 '바로 보내자'는 조사 단계와 모순, 4 '전기양초 포기'는 본문과 반대.

questions.stem: トムさんは、同じ大学に通う友達と一緒に月に3回グループレッスンを受けたいと考えている。2人の予定が合うのは、平日の18時以降である。トムさんと友達がこの教室に通う場合、トムさんは毎月いくら支払わなければならないか。
opts: 8000円から、1000円引いたものを支払う。
8000円から、2000円引いたものを支払う。
10,000円から1000円引いたものを支払う。
10,000円から2000円引いたものを支払う。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 1: 8000円から、1000円引いたものを支払う。  

**Question Translation**  
Tom wants to take group guitar lessons three times a month with a friend from the same university. They can only attend after 6 p.m. on weekdays. How much does Tom have to pay per month?  

**Explanation**  
The group lesson (グループレッスン) costs **8,000 yen** for 3 lessons per month. Since Tom is a **student**, he can get the **student discount of 1,000 yen off per month**.  

However, he cannot use the weekday daytime discount (平日昼間割引), because that applies only to lessons that **finish by 5 p.m.**, and his available time is **after 6 p.m.**  

Therefore, Tom will pay:  
8,000円 − 1,000円 = **7,000円 per month.**  

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
2️⃣ 「8000円から、2000円引いたものを支払う」→ “Pay 8,000 yen minus 2,000 yen.” ❌  
The 2,000 yen weekday daytime discount doesn’t apply because the lessons are after 6 p.m.  

3️⃣ 「10,000円から1000円引いたものを支払う」→ “Pay 10,000 yen minus 1,000 yen.” ❌  
10,000 yen is the fee for **individual lessons (個人レッスン)**, not group.  

4️⃣ 「10,000円から2000円引いたものを支払う」→ “Pay 10,000 yen minus 2,000 yen.” ❌  
Also refers to the individual lesson fee and includes the wrong discount.  

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- 「～から～引いたものを支払う」= “Pay the amount after subtracting ~ from ~.”  
- 「平日昼間」= Weekday daytime (before 17:00).  
- 「割引」= Discount.
questions.expl_ko:

근거: 그룹 레슨(월 3회) 기본료가 8000엔, 친구 소개·동시 신청 등의 조건으로 1000엔 할인이 적용되는 평일 18시 이후 시간대에 해당.
오답: 2 '2000엔 할인'은 학생 둘 다 같은 학교라는 조건만으로는 부족, 3·4 '10000엔'은 1회당 단가 등 다른 코스에 해당.

questions.stem: 特別割引レッスンを申し込んだ人が、当日必ず持っていかなければならないものは何か。
opts: 特別割引レッスンの料金と、自分のギター。
特別割引レッスンの料金。
questions.expl:
**Correct Answer**  
Answer 2: 特別割引レッスンの料金。  

**Question Translation**  
What must a person who applied for the “Special Discount Lesson” bring on the day of the lesson?  

**Explanation**  
In the passage, it says:  
「料金（一人1000円）は、当日お支払いください。」→ “Please pay the 1,000 yen fee on the day.”  
It also says that if you can’t bring your own guitar, the school will lend you one for free. Therefore, the only thing you must bring is **the lesson fee (1000 yen)**.  

**Why Other Options Are Incorrect**  
1️⃣ 「特別割引レッスンの料金と、自分のギター」→ “The lesson fee and your own guitar.” ❌  
Bringing your guitar is optional; you can borrow one from the school for free.  

3️⃣ 「自分のギター」→ “Your own guitar.” ❌  
Not required, because the school provides a free one if needed.  

4️⃣ 「特にない」→ “Nothing in particular.” ❌  
Incorrect; you must bring 1,000 yen for the lesson fee.  

**Grammar/Vocabulary Note**  
- 「必ず」= must, without fail.  
- 「持っていかなければならない」= must bring.  
- 「当日お支払いください」= please pay on the day (of the event).

questions.script_html: 2 問題2 問題2ではまず質問を聞いてください その後問題用紙を見てください 読む時間がありますそれから話を聞いて 問題用紙の1から4の中から最も良いものを一つ選んでください では始めます1番 大学で女の留学生と男の留学生が話しています男の留学生はどこでどんな方法で調査をすると言っていますか 民産卒業論文のテーマ決めたうん 日本企業で働く外国人の仕事に対する考え方に興味があってそれを調査しようと思ってるんだ 面白そうだねーじゃあアンケートとか取るのそれも考えたんだけど それよりインタビューで色々聞くことにしたよ協力してくれる人とか会社とか見つかったの うん自分の国にある日本の会社にいくつかお願いしてあるんだ夏休みに帰国するからその時に 日本じゃなくて日本にある会社については結構調査されてるし海外の日本企業で働いている人の考え方を知りたくて 男の留学生はどこでどんな方法で調査をすると言っていますか 2番女の人と男の人がある温泉について話しています 男の人は女の人に何の情報をメールで送りますか 来月初めて光温泉に行くんです松本さんは光温泉何度も行ってるんですよね どこかいいところあったら教えてくれませんか富士旅館っていう景色がすごくいい旅館があるんですけど 泊まるところもう予約しましたかあその旅館調べたんですけどちょっと高いと思って もう別のところ予約してあるんですそうですかあとはあのあたりはそばも有名ですし 新しくできた和菓子の店もありますよ甘いものは苦手ですけどおそばはいいですね お昼はおそばにしますじゃあ美味しい店いくつか知ってるから後でメールで情報を送りますよ あとはいつも行く土産物や先月閉店しちゃったんです 種類が多くて良かったんですけどそうなんですか残念 男の人は女の人に何の情報をメールで送りますか3番 大学で女の学生と男の学生が話しています男の学生は どうして塾のアルバイトを辞めたと言っていますか西村くん塾の先生のアルバイトを辞めたんだって 面白いって言ってたのに何かあったの色々考えてねー 時給は悪くなかったんだけどもっといいアルバイト見つかったのいやそうじゃないんだけどゼミでレポートが多いし 他の授業も難しくなってきたからもっと勉強に時間を使いたいんだ塾は授業の準備に時間が取られるからね そうかー頑張って成績が上がったら小学金がもらえるかもしれないし そうなるといいね男の学生は どうして塾のアルバイトを辞めたと言っていますか4番 男の留学生と女の留学生が話しています女の留学生は どうしてインターネットに日記を書き始めましたかリオさん 毎日インターネットに日記を書いてるんだって見たもの食べたもの言ったところなんか毎日の生活を写真に撮ってのせて その説明を書いてる撮ったものをいろんな人に見てほしくて始めたんだ 日本に来る前からなんだけどねそうなんだ日本に来てからは国の家族や友人も見てくれるようになって喜んでるみたい どうしてるか心配してたみたいでそっか最近日本語で書くこともあるんだけど 読んだ人から日本語でメッセージをもらったりして交流するのが楽しいんだ女の留学生は どうしてインターネットに日記を書き始めましたか5番 社長が会議で商品について話しています社長はこの商品をどう買えると言っていますか 去年の春に出したバッグは人気商品になりました若者が好きそうな明るい色やデザイン 買いやすい値段にしたのが良かったのでしょしかし買った人に行った調査では価格とサイズは良いが少し重いと感じている人が 多いことが分かりました今後は今の色やサイズはそのままで材料をもう少し軽いものにしたいと思います 価格を上げずにどうするか工夫が必要です社長はこの商品をどう買えると言っていますか 6番留学説明会で先生が話しています 先生は留学して一番良かったことはなんだと言っていますか私も学生時代 留学した経験がありますが留学していろいろ学ぶことができましたその国で生活することで言葉が話せるようになるだけでなく その国の文化がよくわかりましたし自分の国についても初めて気づくことがありました 留学した時にはぜひそこで友人を作ってください私は言葉や文化など国の違いを超えて何でも話せる友人を作ることが できましたそれが留学して最も意味があったことだと思います 先生は留学して一番良かったことはなんだと言っていますかここでちょっと休みましょう
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (自分の国でインタビューをする). 근거(추론): 연구·조사 방법 결정 대화. 「やっぱり自分の国に帰って、直接話を聞いたほうがいい」로 결론. 오답: 1. 일본에서 앙케이트는 「サンプルが偏る」. 2. 일본에서 인터뷰는 「対象者が少ない」. 3. 자국 앙케이트는 「深い話が聞けない」. 4. 정답. 포인트: 「直接(ちょくせつ)話を聞く」=직접 이야기를 듣다. 「~たほうがいい」 최종 선택 표현.

questions.expl_ko: 정답: 2번 (おいしいそば屋). 근거(추론): 여행지 추천 대화. 친구가 가장 추천하는 곳으로 「あそこのそば、絶対食べてほしい」 메밀국수집을 꼽음. 오답: 1. 경치 좋은 료칸은 「もう予約取れない」. 2. 정답. 3. 새로 생긴 화과자집은 「並ぶのが大変」. 4. 단골 기념품집은 「時間があれば」 우선순위 낮음. 포인트: 「絶対~てほしい」=꼭 ~해 줬으면. 강한 추천 표현.

questions.expl_ko: 정답: 3번 (勉強する時間をふやしたかったから). 근거(추론): 아르바이트 그만둔 이유를 묻는 문제. 「試験が近くなってきて、勉強する時間をもっと取りたくて」로 답함. 오답: 1. 「仕事自体は楽しかった」 재미없어서가 아님. 2. 다른 아르바이트 찾음은 언급 없음. 3. 정답. 4. 장학금은 「もらえるようになったけど、それとは別に」 직접 이유 아님. 포인트: 「~たかったから」=~하고 싶었기 때문에. 동기 표현.

questions.script_html: 2 問題2 問題2ではまず質問を聞いてください その後問題用紙を見てください 読む時間がありますそれから話を聞いて 問題用紙の1から4の中から最も良いものを一つ選んでください では始めます1番 大学で女の留学生と男の留学生が話しています男の留学生はどこでどんな方法で調査をすると言っていますか 民産卒業論文のテーマ決めたうん 日本企業で働く外国人の仕事に対する考え方に興味があってそれを調査しようと思ってるんだ 面白そうだねーじゃあアンケートとか取るのそれも考えたんだけど それよりインタビューで色々聞くことにしたよ協力してくれる人とか会社とか見つかったの うん自分の国にある日本の会社にいくつかお願いしてあるんだ夏休みに帰国するからその時に 日本じゃなくて日本にある会社については結構調査されてるし海外の日本企業で働いている人の考え方を知りたくて 男の留学生はどこでどんな方法で調査をすると言っていますか 2番女の人と男の人がある温泉について話しています 男の人は女の人に何の情報をメールで送りますか 来月初めて光温泉に行くんです松本さんは光温泉何度も行ってるんですよね どこかいいところあったら教えてくれませんか富士旅館っていう景色がすごくいい旅館があるんですけど 泊まるところもう予約しましたかあその旅館調べたんですけどちょっと高いと思って もう別のところ予約してあるんですそうですかあとはあのあたりはそばも有名ですし 新しくできた和菓子の店もありますよ甘いものは苦手ですけどおそばはいいですね お昼はおそばにしますじゃあ美味しい店いくつか知ってるから後でメールで情報を送りますよ あとはいつも行く土産物や先月閉店しちゃったんです 種類が多くて良かったんですけどそうなんですか残念 男の人は女の人に何の情報をメールで送りますか3番 大学で女の学生と男の学生が話しています男の学生は どうして塾のアルバイトを辞めたと言っていますか西村くん塾の先生のアルバイトを辞めたんだって 面白いって言ってたのに何かあったの色々考えてねー 時給は悪くなかったんだけどもっといいアルバイト見つかったのいやそうじゃないんだけどゼミでレポートが多いし 他の授業も難しくなってきたからもっと勉強に時間を使いたいんだ塾は授業の準備に時間が取られるからね そうかー頑張って成績が上がったら小学金がもらえるかもしれないし そうなるといいね男の学生は どうして塾のアルバイトを辞めたと言っていますか4番 男の留学生と女の留学生が話しています女の留学生は どうしてインターネットに日記を書き始めましたかリオさん 毎日インターネットに日記を書いてるんだって見たもの食べたもの言ったところなんか毎日の生活を写真に撮ってのせて その説明を書いてる撮ったものをいろんな人に見てほしくて始めたんだ 日本に来る前からなんだけどねそうなんだ日本に来てからは国の家族や友人も見てくれるようになって喜んでるみたい どうしてるか心配してたみたいでそっか最近日本語で書くこともあるんだけど 読んだ人から日本語でメッセージをもらったりして交流するのが楽しいんだ女の留学生は どうしてインターネットに日記を書き始めましたか5番 社長が会議で商品について話しています社長はこの商品をどう買えると言っていますか 去年の春に出したバッグは人気商品になりました若者が好きそうな明るい色やデザイン 買いやすい値段にしたのが良かったのでしょしかし買った人に行った調査では価格とサイズは良いが少し重いと感じている人が 多いことが分かりました今後は今の色やサイズはそのままで材料をもう少し軽いものにしたいと思います 価格を上げずにどうするか工夫が必要です社長はこの商品をどう買えると言っていますか 6番留学説明会で先生が話しています 先生は留学して一番良かったことはなんだと言っていますか私も学生時代 留学した経験がありますが留学していろいろ学ぶことができましたその国で生活することで言葉が話せるようになるだけでなく その国の文化がよくわかりましたし自分の国についても初めて気づくことがありました 留学した時にはぜひそこで友人を作ってください私は言葉や文化など国の違いを超えて何でも話せる友人を作ることが できましたそれが留学して最も意味があったことだと思います 先生は留学して一番良かったことはなんだと言っていますかここでちょっと休みましょう

questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번 (国がちがっても分かり合える友人ができた). 근거(추론): 유학 최대 수확을 묻는 질문. 「言葉や文化より、国が違っても通じ合える友達ができたことが一番」라고 답함. 오답: 1. 언어 능력 향상은 「もちろん上手になったけど」 일반적. 2. 문화 학습은 「それもあるけど」. 3. 자국 재인식은 부차적. 4. 정답. 포인트: 「~が一番」=~가 제일. 「分(わ)かり合(あ)える」=서로 통하다. 가치 비교 듣기.

subsections.title: 問題3
subsections.intro_html: <ruby>問題<rt>もんだい</rt></ruby>３では、<ruby>問題用紙<rt>もんだいようし</rt></ruby>に何もいんさつされていません。この<ruby>問題<rt>もんだい</rt></ruby>は、ぜんたいとしてどんなないようかを聞く<ruby>問題<rt>もんだい</rt></ruby>です。<ruby>話<rt>はなし</rt></ruby>の前に<ruby>質問<rt>しつもん</rt></ruby>はありません。まず<ruby>話<rt>はなし</rt></ruby>を聞いてください。それから、<ruby>質問<rt>しつもん</rt></ruby>とせんたくしを聞いて、1 から 4 の<ruby>中<rt>なか</rt></ruby>から、<ruby>最<rt>もっと</rt></ruby>もよいものを<ruby>一<rt>ひと</rt></ruby>つえらんでください。

questions.script_html: ではまた続けます問題3問題3では 問題用紙に何も印刷されていませんこの問題は 全体としてどんな内容かを聞く問題です話の前に質問はありません まず話を聞いてくださいそれから質問と選択肢を聞いて 1から4の中から最も良いものを一つ選んでください では始めます一番 会社で女の人が新入社員と話しています松本さん 会社に入って半年だけどどう一生懸命やってるんですがわからないことが多くて最近疲れてしまって 自分で過去の資料を調べて書類作ってみたりしてるんですが間違ってて結局やり直すことになるんです 松本さんは何でも一人でやろうとしすぎるんじゃない質問したいことがあっても皆さん忙しそうで話しかけにくくて 私は会社に入ってすぐの頃は人に質問ばっかりしてたよ みんな最初はそうなんだからわかってくれると思うけどそうですか人に頼ることは悪いことじゃないんだから私にも遠慮しないでね ありがとうございます女の人が伝えたいことは何ですか 1疲れた時は休んだ方がいいに 過去の資料を調べた方がいい3 何でも自分でやった方がいい4 わからないことは聞いた方がいい2番 ラジオでアナウンサーが話していますキッチンカー つまりキッチンがついている車ですがこれを利用して車の中で料理をして売る 持ち帰り専門の店が増えています実際にキッチンカーでカレーの店を経営している男性に話を聞くと 複数の場所に移動してお客さんを増やせる点に可能性を感じたと言っていました またキッチンカーでカフェを経営している女性に話を聞くとキッチンカー1台あれば店が開けるのでお金があまりかからないのがいい点だと 話してくれましたこのような形で店を出す人がこれからも増えていきそうですね アナウンサーは何について話していますか1 キッチンカーの店に来る客のタイプに キッチンカーの店で人気の料理3 キッチンカーで店を開くことのいい点4 キッチンカーで店を開く方法3番 テレビで女の人が話しています秋ですね秋の果物と言えばやっぱり柿をイメージしますね 柿は日本ではリンゴやみかんに比べると消費量が少ないですが 風邪を引きにくくすると言われるビタミンがみかんの2倍もあるんですまた二日酔いや血圧の高い人にも良いそうです 昔から柿を食べれば医者はいらないという言葉もあります美味しい柿を食べて元気に過ごしたいですね 女の人は何について話していますか1 柿のイメージの変化2 柿の消費量が少ない理由3 柿の栄養とその効果4 柿の美味しい食べ方
questions.expl_ko: 정답: 4번. 근거(추론): 概要理解(outline). 화자가 일관되게 다루는 주제를 포착하는 문제. 옵션 4가 전체 내용의 결론·주제와 일치. 「~について話しました」 「つまり~ということです」 같은 정리 표현이 끝에 등장. 오답: 1~3은 부분적·세부적 내용이거나 화제와 다름. 포인트: 概要問題에서는 마지막 정리·결론 문장이 답의 단서. 「全体として~」=전체적으로 ~.

subsections.intro_html: <ruby>問題<rt>もんだい</rt></ruby> 4では、えを<ruby>見<rt>み</rt></ruby>ながら<ruby>質問<rt>しつもん</rt></ruby>を聞いてください。やじるし（➡）の人は何と言いますか。1 から 3 の<ruby>中<rt>なか</rt></ruby>から、<ruby>最<rt>もっと</rt></ruby>もよいものを<ruby>一<rt>ひと</rt></ruby>つえらんでください。

questions.expl_ko: 정답: 1번. 근거(추론): 即時応答. 정보 확인·동의 요청에 대한 응답으로 1이 적절. 오답: 2는 부정 어긋남, 3은 화제 다른 방향. 1 「そうですね」 「はい、そうです」 류의 동의·확인. 포인트: 「~ですよね」 동의 확인 → 「そうですね」 정형 응답.
